sections.heading: What is a Running Pace Calculator?
sections.content: Our Running Pace Calculator makes it simple to calculate running pace online for any distance and time. Whether you’re training for a 5K, 10K, half marathon, marathon, or just jogging for fitness, this free tool helps you track your pace per mile or pace per km with ease.

sections.heading: How to Calculate Running Pace
sections.content: Running pace is the time it takes to cover one mile or one kilometer. The formula is simple: Pace = Total Time ÷ Distance. For example: If you run 5K in 25 minutes, your pace is 5:00 min/km (or 8:03 min/mile). If you run a marathon (42.2 km) in 4 hours, your pace is 5:41 min/km. This tool automatically calculates it for you in both metric (km/min) and imperial (mile/min) units.

sections.heading: Running Pace vs Speed – What’s the Difference?
sections.content: Pace = minutes per km or minutes per mile (slower is a higher number). Speed = distance per hour (km/h or mph). Our calculator works as both a pace calculator and a running speed to pace converter.

faq.q: What is a good running pace for beginners?
faq.a: A good starting pace is 7:00–9:00 min/km (11–14 min/mile), depending on fitness level.

faq.q: What is a 5 min/km pace in miles?
faq.a: It equals about 8:03 min/mile or 12 km/h (7.5 mph).

faq.q: How do I calculate pace if I run 10K in 55 minutes?
Can I use this for walking or jogging pace?
faq.a: Yes, it works for walking pace, jogging pace, or running pace in both km and miles.

faq.q: What’s the difference between a Race Pace Calculator and a Jogging Pace Calculator?
faq.a: Race Pace Calculator helps predict competitive race splits, while Jogging Pace Calculator is useful for casual fitness tracking.
